message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin Start button
Clicked Start button
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @soumya.jk can you take point and start sizing this up?
HI Bob, Could you please share more details on the incident
Is it coming from customers everywhere?
Checking reports...
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
can you give an example of specific order or order id?
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
You are right
Gathering more information....
Website showing incorrect product details
*Incident Number INC100018:* Website showing incorrect product details
:star: Incident record created
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
How big is this problem?
Whats teh user impact ?
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
can they place order and right orders are getting delivered?
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
Daniel could you please help us investigate ? SKU #66VCHSJNUP what exactly is this product?  its showing as tank top with a sunglass image
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @soumya.jk What shall we do now?
This is actually going bad- we have to do somethign to stop this
Customers dont know what they are ordering
I ll check that
They are seeing incorrect information on the website.
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
I think its best to put the site on maintencance
What are the orders ? Image or Label ?
Yes go ahead
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
Its very unclear at the moment. But, while we investigate its best to take down the website as we are misleading the customers.
@soumya.jk Could you please discuss this with Bez?
If you approve i will ask the team to put the site on maintenance.
Go Ahead
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
Can we please put the site to maintenance immediately. I have approval from Bez
I am on it
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
@soumya.jk How does it look now?
I can take a look into our services now
Hamed can we do a sweep of the platform in the meanwhile?
What's the impact?
@soumya.jk Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
We don't have any alerts in the system
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Can we involve Tanya, i ll join in a minute
Even i don't see any issue
Site is now under maintenance while we investigate the issue.
*Incident Number INC100098:* Site is now under maintenance while we investigate the issue.
Must be network or platform issue.
Tanya can you please do a sweep of the platform and report any anomalies
This  is the first time , we have to intentionally bring our own Site down
:star: Manage actions and timings
Checking now...
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
Application_Check
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug

Lets focus on a single product Daniel. can you confirm what this product is
<https://uptimelabs-metaverse.slack.com/archives/C0A8P1HKS66/p1768396479586829>
But it's not a platform issue.
Its tough since there are no errors, but yes, as you said, i ll check that
We are trying to understand if its an image issue or a text issue.
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
Shay can you pull the list of recent changes?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
@soumya.jk if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE>
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Hamed thanks for the suggestion. I prefer looking at one data closely. Lets please wait for Daniel to confirm this particular product SKU #66VCHSJNUP
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @soumya.jk
When i track down the data - Its looks fine, the data is comign from redis cache
And that hits the DB - DB is fetching teh data for us
who can tell me : SKU #66VCHSJNUP what exactly is this product? its showing as tank top with a sunglass image
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Lets first identify its a image or text issue
Agreed
Ok here is what i found
Daniel_redis_check
Or it can be image to label mapping
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
tanya_redis_dump_check
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
i am not following. Can we please slow down.
Did the text change or the image?

are we confident SKU #66VCHSJNUP what exactly is this product? its showing as tank top with a sunglass image
its a tank top or sunglass
I made a change in UAT Redis cache - but looks like it has gone to PROD and updated wrong sequence of names in PROD
Thats really bad customer experience
currently site is on maintenance. so no orders are going through
yeah , i follow that
:star: Qualify the problem
Daniel what exactly did you change/
So, the mapping of image and label has changed
:star: Reviewed changes
Can you share full details of the changes?
My change was to update the names in UAT - But its not the same order in PROD
Shay did any other change happen in the last 1 hour?
No, but cache refrshed in last 1 hour
hence we were serving from cache
Shay which cache refreshed?
Redis cache have teh data, but that got refreshed
Daniel can you share the query? did you update the names of the product against their id?
yes

Change ID: CHG89412
Owner: Daniel
Service: CartRedis
Environment: UAT
Version Deployed: 2.7.4
Time Of Change: 27 hours ago
Description: Execute a cache-refresh script in UAT to verify that product names in Redis match the latest product-name records in the UAT database
Its just that instead of PROD , it ha sgone to UAT
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
I think we should make some decisions now
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
we are runnign out of time
It your call @soumya.jk
Do you have any ETA ?i have calls with stake holders now
Does the database currently have the correct data?
No -
we have identified that the redis database update has caused the issue.
*Incident Number INC100018:* we have identified that the redis database update has caused the issue.
Daniel can you put the correct data base to redis database?
We will have to repopulate the data , as i told Dan's query has updated wrong names in DB- Dan suggestion wont fix
Who can put the correct data back to database?
We are workign on updating the database
i can
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
Tanya please go ahead. Do you have an ETA?
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
Hamed can you review Tanya’s changes
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@soumya.jk please verify that all steps have been completed
Yes, this looks good for me
Are we good to proceed @soumya.jk?
Proceed
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
Step 1 is now complete
We are working on updating the database with the correct details
*Incident Number INC100048:* We are working on updating the database with the correct details
Moving on to step 2
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....

Flush completed
Rehydrate completed
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
Can we verify if the product details are now correct without bringng the website back online?
I dont want bad orders to go through if its still not correct.
Maintenance page removal in progress...
Daniel hold on
@soumya.jk Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
sorry, i did chk that
it looks fine for me
Post_recovery
We are LIVE for orders back again!
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
great all looks okay
Bob can you please check if orders are okay
yes, we are looking good
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
calls have reduced
we have updated the database and taken the site out of maintenance
*Incident Number INC100038:* we have updated the database and taken the site out of maintenance
we are monitoring the platform
*Incident Number INC100018:* we are monitoring the platform
Bob can you report back in 2 mins if any more complaints
sure
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Looks like we are back to business
:star: Track actions and time box them
Well done everyone
Thanks @soumya.jk and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
_thanks Soumya - that was intense

Thank you for playing, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit I am ready when you want to start. I am ready button
appreciate your readiness

For every order which happened during the issue Bob: If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
resolve the incident.
*Incident Number INC100048:* resolve the incident.
thanks
thanks all
thanks Bez
thanks
Clicked Start_feedback button
Submission successful
